plan.name: Convex-Persisted Polished Chat View and Agent-Ready Roadmap
plan.goal: Build a polished Convex-backed chat experience with agent-ready context, replies, and date navigation.

features.name: Dedicated Polished Chat Route and Layout
features.description: Create a dedicated SvelteKit `/chat` route using Svelte/SvelteKit idioms (`+page.svelte`, Svelte 5 state/props patterns, generated `$types` if load functions are introduced). Include an agent header, scrollable long-form message history, message composer, and responsive web-first layout that anticipates later mobile app use. The upper-left header area shows the agent icon and current conversation date control. Keep route data serializable if SvelteKit load functions are added, and prioritize fast initial render, smooth scrolling, stable layout, hover states, and mouse/keyboard interactions.

features.name: Convex Conversation Persistence and Message Metadata
features.description: Use and extend the existing Convex backend for conversation sessions and messages so chat history can be saved, loaded quickly in chronological order, and scoped by user/session. Define schema changes with `defineSchema`, `defineTable`, and `v` validators; implement public queries/mutations with argument validators and explicit access control; and use `.withIndex()` plus bounded/paginated reads for message history. Include metadata for role, status, timestamps, reply target, automated status/system messages, and future agent context events while keeping reusable business logic in plain TypeScript helpers where practical.

features.name: Developer Chat Data Reset and Mock Seeding Tools
features.description: Provide an easy manual testing workflow to wipe chat conversation/message data between sessions and populate Convex with realistic mock messages from both user and agent across multiple dates. Implement wipe/seed paths as validated, clearly development-only utilities, using indexed Convex queries rather than unbounded table scans where data can grow. Tools must be clearly marked as development/testing utilities and avoid destructive ambiguity.

features.name: Placeholder Agent Reply Flow
features.description: When the user sends a message, persist/display it immediately and generate a varied placeholder assistant response through the same validated Convex mutation/interface boundary expected by a future real agent integration. Replies should support pending/completed UI state, use deterministic/testable placeholder selection, avoid real model/API calls, and update Svelte state from Convex-backed data rather than maintaining a second canonical history.

features.name: Hybrid Agent Context Scoping Abstraction
features.description: Implement a testable TypeScript context-building layer. For normal sends, include the newest user message plus a fixed recent window. For reply-to sends, include the replied-to message, surrounding messages near that reply target, and the recent tail. Use indexed/bounded Convex reads where database access is needed, centralize window-size configuration, keep the default payload from including full history, and expose an abstraction ready for a future agent to request broader database context.

features.name: Automated Context Refresh Status Event
features.description: Model the future agent database-refresh behavior by providing an explicit backend/interface event path that inserts an automated non-agent message saying the agent is refreshing themselves on the topic whenever broader context is requested. Implement the callable path with Convex validators and authorization, distinguish the record from assistant-authored messages in schema/UI metadata, and ensure this automated write cannot trigger normal placeholder replies.

features.name: Polished Reply-to Message UX with Gestures
features.description: Allow users to choose a specific prior message to reply to using polished interactions inspired by iMessage. Include mouse click controls, hover states, keyboard-accessible controls, and gesture-ready behavior for future mobile use. Show a quoted/linked preview in the composer, allow clearing it, persist reply metadata through the Convex message contract, and render the association in history without breaking chronological ordering.

features.name: Sequential User Message Response Organization Stub
features.description: Detect runs of multiple consecutive user messages before an assistant response and represent them as grouped input for a future small model. For now, use a deterministic TypeScript stub that can display/structure placeholder assistant responses as if they address individual messages where appropriate, while preserving the underlying persisted message records and visible chronological history.

features.name: Polished Conversation Date Dropdown Navigation
features.description: Implement the current-date control beneath the agent icon as a polished dropdown listing available conversation dates from persisted data. Derive date groups from bounded/paginated Convex-backed message data, keep Svelte UI state serializable and local to the route/component where appropriate, and selecting a date smoothly scrolls/jumps to that day’s exchanges without flicker/pop-in so users can review and reply to older messages. Seeded mock data should make this easy to test.

features.name: Interaction, Performance, Accessibility, and Regression Coverage
features.description: Add or update tests and checks for Convex message operations, development wipe/seed utilities, context scoping, reply metadata, placeholder behavior, date grouping/navigation logic, smooth loading assumptions, keyboard/mouse/hover interactions, and accessible controls. Include coverage for Convex validators/auth/indexed access where feasible, SvelteKit route behavior where practical, and ensure build, lint, type checks, and applicable tests pass.

milestones.name: Convex Chat Foundation and Test Data Tools
milestones.description: Establish the dedicated SvelteKit chat route, Convex persistence schema/API shape, indexed/paginated message loading, and manual development utilities to wipe and seed chat data.

milestones.name: Agent-Ready Send Pipeline
milestones.description: Implement sending, placeholder replies via future-agent-ready validated interfaces, hybrid scoped context construction, and automated context-refresh status events without real model calls.

milestones.name: Polished Reply and Multi-Message Interaction
milestones.description: Add iMessage-like reply targeting with mouse, hover, keyboard, and gesture-ready interaction, plus deterministic TypeScript scaffolding for organizing responses to multiple consecutive user messages while preserving persisted history.

milestones.name: Date Navigation, Performance, and Hardening
milestones.description: Add polished date-based navigation using persisted/seeded data and final validation for smoothness, interaction quality, accessibility, backend utilities, Convex index usage, and regression safety.

assertions.description: A dedicated SvelteKit chat view is reachable through the app and contains recognizable agent header, message history, and composer regions.
The chat view presents messages as one continuous chronological conversation history without relying on React-style state patterns.
The chat layout remains usable on common mobile and desktop viewport widths without horizontal overflow and with design choices compatible with future mobile app adaptation.
Persisted chat messages render into Svelte quickly with stable layout, avoiding visible pop-in/flicker during normal seeded test loads; any SvelteKit load data introduced is serializable.
The message history supports smooth scrolling behavior without layout jank during normal use, date jumps, and reply target selection.
User, assistant, and automated status messages can be saved to and reloaded from Convex for a conversation session via validated queries/mutations.
Reloaded messages appear in stable chronological order within their conversation session using timestamp-aware indexed query ordering.
Message records can distinguish author/role, status, timestamps, reply target, and automated refresh/status messages through explicit Convex schema fields validated with `v` validators.
Convex queries and mutations validate arguments and enforce authenticated user/session scoping so one user/session cannot read or mutate another user's chat data.
Conversation queries use `.withIndex()` and bounded or paginated reads rather than unbounded scans so seeded chat histories load efficiently for frequent manual testing.
A manual development/testing workflow can wipe existing chat sessions/messages so the tester can start fresh between sessions.
A manual development/testing workflow can populate Convex with realistic mock user and agent messages through validated development-only code paths.
Destructive wipe/seed utilities are clearly development-only or otherwise gated to prevent accidental production use.
Seeded mock data can span multiple dates so date dropdown navigation can be manually tested immediately.
Submitting composer text appends a user message, clears the composer, and produces a visible assistant placeholder reply persisted through the Convex-backed flow.
Placeholder assistant replies vary across multiple user sends from a maintained phrase set rather than always returning identical text.
The UI can represent waiting/pending assistant response state and settle into a completed response state from persisted status metadata.

Placeholder replies and grouped-message handling flow through documented TypeScript interfaces/events that can later be replaced by real agent/model integration without rewriting the chat UI contract.
For a normal send, the constructed future-agent payload includes the newest user message and a fixed recent message window.
For a reply-to send, the constructed future-agent payload includes the replied-to message, nearby surrounding messages around it, and bounded recent context.
The default future-agent payload does not include the entire conversation history when older messages exist outside the configured context windows.
Recent-window size and reply-surrounding-window size are centralized/configurable enough to tune later without UI rewrites.
When broader context refresh is triggered, the conversation displays an automated message that says the agent is working on refreshing themselves on the topic.
The context-refresh message is visibly/systemically distinguishable from a user message and from an assistant-authored response.
There is an explicit callable Convex/helper path for future agent code to request broader message database context and emit the refresh status message.
Automated context-refresh status messages do not recursively trigger placeholder assistant replies or normal user-send behavior.
Users can select a specific existing message as the target of their next reply.
The composer shows the selected reply target preview and lets the user cancel/clear it before sending.
Sent replies render with a visible reference to the message they replied to.
Sending a reply preserves reply metadata in Convex and passes the reply-target context into the hybrid context builder.
Reply controls expose clear hover states, can be activated by mouse click, and are operable via keyboard in manual/user testing.
Reply interaction architecture and styling are gesture-ready for future mobile adaptation, even if full mobile-app validation is deferred.
Multiple consecutive user messages before an assistant reply can be identified as a group without changing their visible chronological history.
The placeholder response flow can produce a structured response that appears to address individual messages in a consecutive user-message group.
Grouping logic never merges, deletes, or reorders the underlying user messages in the displayed chat history or persisted Convex records.
The chat header displays the date currently represented by the conversation viewport/date context beneath the agent icon.
The date control opens a dropdown listing dates that have messages in the current conversation, derived from persisted message timestamps.
Selecting a date from the dropdown smoothly scrolls or jumps the message history to that date’s exchanges.
Date navigation does not cause obvious pop-in, flicker, or unstable relayout during seeded manual tests.
After navigating to an older date, the user can select an older message as a reply target and send a reply from the current composer.
Automated tests cover hybrid context scoping for normal sends, reply-to sends, and exclusion of full-history payloads by default.

Tests or clearly documented manual validation cover Convex message persistence plus development wipe and seed workflows, including validators, auth/scoping, and index-aware query behavior where feasible.
Manual/user testing validates keyboard interaction, mouse click interaction, and hover states for composer, reply controls, and date dropdown.
Composer, reply controls, and date dropdown are keyboard accessible and expose useful labels/roles for assistive technologies.
The project lint, SvelteKit type-check, applicable test suite, and production build complete successfully after implementation.

tests: cd app && npm test -- --run (if a test script is added)
For Convex code, verify public queries/mutations/actions have `args` validators and any user-owned data paths perform authorization/scoping checks.
Manually validate Convex wipe and seed workflow in a development environment
Manually validate keyboard, mouse click, and hover interactions for composer, reply controls, and date dropdown
safetyChecks: Review `.dev/docs/sveltekit-context7.md` and `.dev/docs/convex-context7.md` before implementing stack-sensitive changes; refresh with Context7 if version-sensitive behavior is unclear.
Review Convex schema/function changes for `v` validators, `userId`/`sessionId` scoping, and authenticated authorization so one user cannot read or mutate another user's conversations
Review Convex message reads for `.withIndex()`, bounded `.take(...)`, or pagination instead of unbounded scans
Verify SvelteKit load data, if introduced, is serializable and typed with generated `$types` where applicable
Verify chat wipe/seed utilities are development-only, clearly labeled, and not exposed as an unsafe production action
Verify no real API keys, model calls, or secret material are introduced for placeholder agent replies
Verify automated context-refresh messages cannot be spoofed through normal user composer input as assistant-authored messages
Check seeded/performance test histories for visible flicker, pop-in, layout shifts, or scroll jank during normal manual testing

plan.originalGoal: "For this project I want to get started with one of the most fundamental areas of user interaction, which is building up the chat feature. Now in this SvelteKit app we'll want to have a view that is dedicated to the chat. Here's some of the functionality and features that the chat will have that I want us to build a road map around creating: 1. The chat is between the user and an agent. For now without the agent being hooked up, let's just have the chat reply back to the user with some variety of placeholder phrases after the user writes something to them. That's just for now as we build it out. 2. Now crucially because this is going to be going to an agent, we want the messages from the chat and the most recent context to be scoped to the most recent message that the user is sending to the agent. The chat is going to present as just one long chat history but again the context of what's sent to the agent is scoped just to recent things. If the agent reads the most recent message plus surrounding context and feels like they need additional context, they're able to query the messages database. When the agent does that, an automated message is sent to the user, not written by the agent, that just says that the agent is working on refreshing themselves on the topic. 3. In terms of additional features, users reply to a message in particular, much like how a user could in iMessage. When a user replies to a specific message, not only is their reply sent but the message they're replying to and the other messages surrounding the one that they're replying to are also sent to the agent. When a user sends multiple messages in a row, all after one another, some small model can help organize the agent's response and can make it appear like the agent is replying to individual user messages. 4. Within the chat view, to the upper left, underneath the icon for the agent that the user is talking to, there should be the current date of the conversation. That current date serves as a drop-down menu and lets the user scroll through previous dates of the conversation and jump back to earlier exchanges, mostly so they can reply to older messages."

completedItems: Implemented and committed a dedicated `/chat` SvelteKit route with recognizable agent header/date control, scrollable chronological message history, reply controls, composer, loading/empty/error states, and dev-only seed/clear buttons.
Connected the chat route to Convex via `convex-svelte` using Svelte 5 runes rather than React-style state patterns.
Added anonymous browser session scoping for local chat persistence so message reads/writes are scoped per authenticated user or local session.
Added indexed, bounded Convex message reads and scoped mutations with validators for listing, sending, seeding, clearing, and automated context refresh placeholders.
Added `chatContext.ts` helper for agent-ready context windows, reply context IDs, and grouped consecutive user messages.

Polished responsive styling for desktop and mobile widths with stable full-height layout, smooth scroll behavior, sticky date dividers, keyboard date selection, hover/focus states, reduced-motion handling, and OKLCH/tinted color choices.
Committed changes in git commit `6037dbd` (`feat(chat): build polished Convex chat route`).

Wired `sendMessage` through the context builder and stored context metadata on persisted messages.

validationAssertionsTouched.notes: Header date button displays the active viewport/date context and updates from scroll or selected date.
Dropdown lists grouped dates with message counts from the bounded Convex-backed message query data.
Selecting a date closes the menu and smoothly scrolls to the first message for that day.
Jump uses scrollIntoView plus box-shadow-only highlight animation, avoiding remounts or layout animation. Manual seeded visual validation still recommended.
Older day messages remain normal message rows with existing reply buttons after date jumps, allowing reply target selection and composer send.

completedItems: Enhanced the chat header date control in `app/src/routes/chat/+page.svelte` with a clearer current viewport date label, message-count summary, `aria-controls`, and animated chevron state.
Kept date groups derived from persisted Convex message timestamps already returned by the bounded indexed `api.chat.listMessages` query, without introducing non-serializable load state.
Improved initial viewport positioning so loaded conversations settle at the latest messages and the header date matches the represented viewport context.
Changed date jumps to target the sticky day divider for more stable scroll positioning, preserving smooth scroll and highlight feedback without relayout-heavy behavior.
Added outside-pointer closing for the date dropdown while preserving Escape and focus-out closing behavior.
Verified older-message reply flow remains wired after date navigation because jump only changes scroll/active date state and reply target selection/composer state remains local to the route.
unfinishedItems: Manual Convex wipe/seed workflow and hands-on keyboard/mouse/hover testing were not performed in a live browser during this worker pass.

issuesDiscovered: Mission complete

findings.description: The milestone calls for Convex persistence, but the chat route is entirely local state with hard-coded initial messages and local-only sends. There are no Convex chat queries/mutations for saving or reloading user/assistant/system messages, so messages are lost on reload and backend persistence cannot be validated.

No manual development/testing wipe workflow for chat sessions/messages exists. The only Convex function found is tasks.health, and there are no mutations/actions/scripts to delete existing chat messages between manual test sessions.
No manual seed workflow exists to populate Convex with realistic mock user/agent chat data. Consequently seeded multi-date histories for immediate date-navigation testing are also absent.
Because wipe/seed utilities are not implemented, the required development-only gating/safety labeling for destructive utilities cannot be verified.

The current message schema cannot represent required chat metadata such as reply target/reply relationship or automated refresh/status-message attribution beyond a generic role/status. This is insufficient for the milestone's persistence shape and future reply/context flows.

The chat UI date control is a non-functional button: it does not open a dropdown, list available conversation dates, or jump/scroll to messages from a selected date. The route only renders one hard-coded sticky day divider.
Submitting a message only appends the user's local message. It does not produce any assistant placeholder reply and does not persist through a backend flow.
Placeholder assistant reply variety is not implemented because no placeholder assistant reply is generated after user sends.
`npm test -- --run` fails because no test script exists. This is acceptable only if no tests were expected for this milestone, but it means automated test coverage is currently absent.

Convex chat queries/mutations are scoped to a hard-coded DEV_AUTH_ID and do not call ctx.auth/getUserIdentity or accept/authorize a real session/user boundary. Any caller of listMessages/sendMessage/seedDevData/clearDevData operates on the same shared dev-local-chat-user data, so one user/session isolation is not enforced.

Destructive seed/wipe Convex mutations are public exported mutations and are gated only by a literal confirmation string. They are callable outside the Svelte dev-only UI path and do not verify the Convex deployment/environment, authenticated admin/dev identity, or any server-side development-only condition.

The current date control is rendered as a button but has no dropdown state, date list, or click handler. There is no implemented route-shell behavior for listing persisted message dates or jumping to a selected date.
Because the date dropdown/list is not implemented, selecting a date cannot smoothly scroll/jump to that date's exchanges, and replying after a date jump cannot be validated.

No explicit context-builder module/interface was found for constructing future-agent payloads with bounded recent context or hybrid reply context. sendMessage persists a message and placeholder reply directly without producing the required normal/reply context payload.
Reply metadata can be saved, but there is no hybrid context construction that includes the replied-to message, surrounding messages, and bounded recent context for future agent handoff.
No automated context-refresh callable/helper path was found that future agent code can invoke to query broader message database context and emit the required refresh status message.
No automated refresh/status message insertion behavior was found, so the required user-visible text indicating that the agent is working on refreshing themselves on the topic is absent.
No grouping logic was found to identify multiple consecutive user messages before an assistant reply or produce structured placeholder responses that address individual grouped messages.
No automated tests were found or runnable via npm test; app/package.json has no test script. Required unit coverage for context scoping is absent.
npm run check and npm run build passed. npm run lint completed with exit code 0 but emitted four warnings for unused eslint-disable directives in generated Convex files.
Reply controls are only rendered for assistant messages. The requirement says users can reply to a specific existing message; if that includes user/system messages, the current UI is too narrow.

Date dropdown/listbox is now present, but the header date does not update to the selected/jumped date or viewport date. `currentDate` is derived from `messages.at(-1)` only, so after selecting an older date the button still displays the newest message date rather than the conversation date represented by the viewport/date context.

Convex chat queries/mutations remain scoped to a hard-coded `DEV_AUTH_ID` and do not authenticate via `ctx.auth` or enforce per-user/per-session authorization. Any caller uses the same shared dev-local-chat-user data, so one user/session isolation is not enforced.

Destructive wipe/seed Convex mutations remain publicly exported and gated only by literal confirmation strings. There is no server-side deployment/environment or authenticated dev/admin check preventing accidental production use.

No explicit context-builder module/interface was found for constructing future-agent payloads with bounded recent context. `sendMessage` directly persists the user message and placeholder reply without constructing a normal recent-window payload.
Reply metadata can be persisted, but no hybrid context builder includes the replied-to message, surrounding messages, and bounded recent context for future agent handoff.
Because no context builder exists, there is no evidence that default future-agent payloads exclude the full conversation history when older messages exist outside configured windows.
No centralized/configurable recent-window or reply-surrounding-window configuration was found for context construction.
No automated context-refresh callable/helper path was found that future agent code can invoke to request broader database context and emit the refresh status message.
No implementation inserts the required automated status message text saying the agent is working on refreshing themselves on the topic.
No grouping logic was found to identify multiple consecutive user messages before an assistant reply or to produce structured placeholder responses addressing individual messages in such a group.
No automated tests cover hybrid context scoping for normal sends, reply-to sends, or excluding full-history payloads. `npm test -- --run` fails because `app/package.json` has no `test` script.
`npm run check` and `npm run build` pass. `npm run lint` exits 0 but emits four existing warnings for unused eslint-disable directives in generated Convex files.

The latest compact header CSS uses `container-type` on `.chat-header`, but the `@container` rule attempts to style `.chat-header` itself. A container query only applies to descendants of the queried container, so the nested `.chat-header { align-items: flex-start; gap: ... }` rule is ineffective. Descendant header action rules still apply, so this is a minor responsive-polish issue rather than a current blocker.

`npm run check` and `npm run build` pass. `npm run lint` exits 0 but emits four existing warnings for unused eslint-disable directives in generated Convex files. Svelte MCP Autofixer was run for the changed chat route and returned advisory suggestions about assigning `activeDateKey` inside `$effect`; this was treated as non-blocking because the current route type-checks/builds and the effect is synchronizing local viewport state.

The current milestone requires Convex chat persistence, but the chat route still uses hard-coded local demo messages and local component state only. There are no Convex chat queries or mutations for saving/reloading chat messages, so messages are lost on reload and persisted chat behavior cannot satisfy the milestone.

No Convex API exists to list/reload messages in stable chronological order for a conversation session. The only Convex function found is `tasks.health`; the existing schema indexes are not exercised by any chat query.

The persisted message schema still lacks explicit fields for reply target and automated refresh/status attribution. It has role/status/timestamp, but cannot fully distinguish required reply relationships or automated refresh event metadata through explicit validated schema fields.

No chat queries or mutations enforce authenticated user/session access control. Schema contains userId/sessionId fields, but without implemented Convex functions there is no validated, authorized path proving one user/session cannot read or mutate another user's conversations.

No implemented conversation message query uses `.withIndex()`, bounded `.take(...)`, or pagination for seeded chat histories. Existing chat data is local and Convex message indexes are unused by application code.
Manual development wipe tooling for chat sessions/messages is absent. There is no script, Convex mutation, or documented workflow to reset chat data between test sessions.
Manual development seed tooling for realistic mock chat data is absent. There is no workflow to populate Convex with user/agent messages for manual testing.
Because destructive wipe/seed utilities are not implemented, the required development-only gating and safety labeling cannot be verified.
No seed data workflow exists to create messages spanning multiple dates, so date navigation cannot be immediately tested from seeded Convex data.
`npm test -- --run` fails because `app/package.json` has no `test` script. Type-check, lint, and build pass, but the test procedure remains unavailable.

Public chat query/mutation do not use Convex authentication or per-user authorization. Instead they hard-code `DEV_AUTH_ID = 'dev-local-chat-user'`, so every client shares the same conversation and can read/write the same messages. This violates the milestone requirement that one user/session cannot read or mutate another user's chat data.

Convex persistence now partially works: `chat:sendMessage` creates user and assistant messages and `chat:listMessages` reloads them in timestamp order. However the implementation only supports a single implicit latest dev session and has no session argument or user/session scoping, so it is not a complete conversation-session persistence shape for the milestone.

Message schema still lacks explicit fields for reply target and automated refresh/status attribution. Existing fields cover role/status/timestamp, but not the required reply relationship or automated refresh event distinction through explicit validated schema fields.
Manual development wipe tooling for chat sessions/messages is absent. No Convex mutation, script, or documented workflow exists to reset chat data between test sessions.
Manual development seed tooling for realistic mock chat data is absent. There is no development-only workflow to populate Convex with realistic user/agent messages.
Because wipe/seed utilities are not implemented, the required development-only gating and safety labeling for destructive utilities cannot be verified.
No seed workflow exists to create messages spanning multiple dates, so date dropdown/navigation testing from seeded Convex data is not supported.
`npm run lint` fails because generated Convex files under `app/src/convex/_generated/` do not match the repository's Prettier configuration. This blocks the required quality command.
`npm test -- --run` fails because `app/package.json` has no `test` script. This remains a procedure gap, although the procedure says to run it only if a test script is added.

Public chat query/mutation still do not use Convex authentication or per-user authorization. They hard-code `DEV_AUTH_ID = 'dev-local-chat-user'`, so every client shares the same conversation and can read/write/clear/seed the same dev user's messages. This violates the milestone requirement that one user/session cannot read or mutate another user's chat data.

Destructive wipe/seed utilities are named as dev tools and require literal confirmation strings, but they are public Convex mutations and are exposed directly in the chat UI without an environment/deployment guard. If this code is deployed as-is, any visitor can clear or reseed the shared chat database.

Message schema still lacks explicit fields for reply target and automated refresh/status attribution. Existing fields cover role/status/timestamp, but not the required reply relationship or automated refresh event distinction through explicit validated schema fields.
`npm test -- --run` fails because `app/package.json` has no `test` script. This remains a procedure gap, although the procedure says to run it only if a test script is added.

Svelte Autofixer reviewed the changed chat route and returned advisory suggestions about `activeDateKey` being assigned inside `$effect`, function calls from effects, and possible alternatives to `bind:this`. These are not milestone blockers: the effect is synchronizing viewport/date state, the referenced function does not assign state, and the current DOM binding supports scroll/date-jump behavior without violating the route-shell assertions.

Worker-reported quality commands passed, but lint still emits existing generated Convex unused eslint-disable warnings. This does not block the milestone because ESLint exits successfully and the warnings are in generated files.

The latest worker handoff for `chat-route-shell` made no code changes and correctly identified the durable assignment as stale; it did not reopen any previously verified route-shell assertions. Current repository diffs still reflect the already-reviewed milestone implementation rather than new route-shell work.

Svelte Autofixer advisory items from the prior pass remain non-blocking: `activeDateKey` is synchronized in an effect for viewport/date state, and `bind:this` supports scroll/date-jump behavior. No new Svelte changes were introduced by the latest handoff that would require a new repair request.

Anonymous chat scoping relies on possession of the browser-generated clientSessionId rather than a server-issued/HttpOnly anonymous identity. This is acceptable for local unauthenticated development scope but should be replaced or hardened before treating anonymous sessions as production security boundaries.

Validation was static code review only: I did not run lint/check/build/test or manually exercise Convex seed/clear flows because validator procedure defaults defer commands/live testing unless explicitly requested. Worker handoffs did not include command output evidence.

Reply-context construction cannot include the replied-to message or surrounding messages when the reply target is older than the bounded recent fetch. sendMessage validates replyingToMessage with assertMessageInSession, but then buildAgentContextPayload only receives getRecentMessages() output. If the user jumps to an older date and replies to an old message outside that recent bounded slice, targetIndex is -1 and replyContextMessageIds remains empty, violating the required hybrid old-target + surrounding + recent context behavior.

No automated tests were added for hybrid context scoping. Repository inspection found no test/spec files covering normal sends, reply-to sends, or exclusion of full-history payloads by default, despite this assertion requiring automated coverage.

Grouped placeholder text acknowledges that multiple user messages are being tracked, but does not actually address individual messages in the group. This may be acceptable as placeholder behavior, but it falls short of the stronger UX wording that the response can appear to address individual messages in a consecutive group.

The handoff did not provide evidence that npm run check, npm run lint, npm run build, or tests passed for this milestone. Per validator workflow I did not run automated checks by default, so this assertion remains unverified from available evidence.

`npm run lint` completed with exit code 0, but ESLint reported four warnings for unused eslint-disable directives in generated Convex files. These are generated-file cleanup warnings and do not block the milestone assertions.

Svelte MCP server was connected, but the project-specific full-file Autofixer advisory pass was not completed in this validator run due to tool argument handoff limitations. Static Svelte review of the touched route did not reveal Svelte 5 rune/event/a11y regressions.

Validation commands passed. `npm run lint` exits successfully but continues to report four existing warnings for unused eslint-disable directives in generated Convex files; this is unchanged and non-blocking.
No Svelte files changed in the latest worker commit, so the project-specific Svelte Autofixer pass had no touched Svelte files to review.
